hypothesis: When paired coding-agent task outcomes share repository-level treatment heterogeneity, task-independent confidence intervals will fall below 95% coverage while repository-clustered resampling will stay materially closer to nominal coverage.
claimBoundary: The evidence supports estimator selection for the declared hierarchical Bernoulli simulation only; it does not measure a named benchmark, prove universal repository dependence, or replace empirical correlation diagnostics on production evaluation data.

baselines: Percentile bootstrap resampling individual task deltas
controls: Zero repository-level treatment heterogeneity independence control
